meta.name: PHQ-4 Patient Health Questionnaire
meta.description: The PHQ-4 is a 4-item ultra-brief screening tool that combines the PHQ-2 (depression) and GAD-2 (anxiety) to provide a quick assessment of both depressive and anxiety symptoms. It is useful as a first-step screening in primary care and general population surveys.
meta.author: Kroenke, Spitzer, Williams & Löwe (2009)
meta.reference: Kroenke K, Spitzer RL, Williams JBW, Löwe B. An ultra-brief screening scale for anxiety and depression: the PHQ-4. Psychosomatics. 2009;50(6):613-621.
meta.instruction: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by the following problems?

questions.text: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
Not being able to stop or control worrying

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your total score is within the normal range, indicating no significant symptoms of anxiety or depression over the past two weeks. Your current psychological well-being appears to be in a healthy state.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a balanced lifestyle with regular exercise, adequate sleep, and social engagement
Keep practicing healthy stress management habits that support your overall emotional well-being
Remember that brief periods of stress or low mood are a normal part of life and typically resolve naturally

ranges.label: Mild Distress
ranges.description: You may be experiencing mild psychological distress involving some anxiety or depressive symptoms. These symptoms are common and may reflect recent stress, but monitoring them over time is a good practice.
suggestions: Pay closer attention to your emotional well-being and notice patterns in when you feel most stressed or low
Practice daily stress management techniques such as deep breathing, mindfulness, or gentle physical exercise
Maintain regular sleep and exercise routines, as both have a strong positive impact on mood and anxiety
If symptoms continue or worsen, consider completing the full PHQ-9 or GAD-7 for a more detailed assessment

ranges.label: Moderate Distress
ranges.description: Your score indicates moderate psychological distress that may involve both anxiety and depressive symptoms affecting your daily life. A more detailed assessment is recommended to better understand your specific needs.
suggestions: Consider completing the full PHQ-9 and GAD-7 assessments to get a more detailed picture of your symptoms
Consult a healthcare professional for a comprehensive evaluation to determine whether treatment would be helpful
Practice daily relaxation and stress management techniques to help reduce your current level of distress
Maintain and strengthen your social connections and support networks, as social support is protective
Establish consistent daily routines for sleep, meals, and activity to provide structure and stability

ranges.label: Severe Distress
ranges.description: Your score suggests significant psychological distress that is likely substantially impacting your daily functioning and quality of life. Professional evaluation and support are strongly recommended at this level.
suggestions: Seek consultation with a mental health professional promptly for a thorough evaluation and treatment planning
Complete the full PHQ-9 and GAD-7 assessments to help identify whether depression, anxiety, or both are primary concerns
Ensure you have a reliable support system of trusted people you can reach out to when you are struggling
Remember that effective treatments exist for both anxiety and depression, and seeking help is a sign of strength
